name: George Pickens
headline: Pickens' Dallas Breakout Is Real, Not a Mirage
body:
Pickens turned his first year in Dallas into a top-3 finish in our WR model, hauling in 93 of 137 targets for 1,429 yards and nine scores across all 17 games. The efficiency drove it: a 0.64 EPA per play and a 63.5% success rate dwarfed his Pittsburgh marks (0.27 and 41.7% in 2024), while 2.46 yards per route and a 0.235 target-per-route rate confirmed he was no longer a boom-or-bust deep threat. A 22.6% target share and 23 red-zone looks, even sharing a backfield-style workload with CeeDee Lamb, gave him a real floor.

The questions are off the field. Pickens is angling for a new deal and skipped voluntary work, and a sturdier Dallas defense could trim pass volume. Still, he profiles as a back-end WR1 going around the second-round turn, and his per-game ceiling justifies that price.
